title: thuja occidentalis f. pumila
intro: Thuja occidentalis f. pumila, also known as eastern white cedar or arborvitae, is a small, evergreen shrub native to eastern North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are derived from the essential oils contained within its leaves and stems. The health benefits of Thuja occidentalis f. pumila include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and fight off infections, making it a popular natural remedy for respiratory issues and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Thuja occidentalis f. pumila include teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat various health issues, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using this plant medicinally.
